characters.name: Amalockh
characters.description: AmalockhSpeciesMany-armed monsterGenderMaleEnemiesThe whole townStatusDeceased (presumably) Amalockh(also mispronounced asAmalochk) is a gigantic, many-armed monster who was summoned byProntousing theAnimal Chain. He resides in a giant tomb, that features small pillars, boxes, and turntables on the side. The tombstone lies in theGraveyard, a location that is rarely seen in theseries. Unlike many characters in12 oz. Mouse, Amalockh isn't animated in a crudely drawn art style as his design is very similar to the character design ofAqua Teen Hunger Force, anotherAdult Swimshow that was co-created by the series' creatorMatt Maiellaro.

characters.name: Archeus
characters.description: ArcheusSpeciesOrb of energyGenderFemaleAlliancesMouse FitzgeraldEnemiesSharkRectangular BusinessmanStatusAliveAppearances"Prolegomenon" (only appearance) Rectangular Businessman Archeusis a female, pink-glowing orb who confrontsMouse Fitzgeraldin "Prolegomenon". While visually similar to a combination ofCJ Muff's orb form and theMusic Notesfrom "Booger Haze", she is presumed to be a separate entity or a fusion of the two beings.

characters.name: Aria
characters.description: AriaSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleAlliancesProfessor Wilx(formerly)Mouse Fitzgerald(formerly)Eye(formerly)Golden Joe(formerly)Peanut CopThe Kid(formerly)Skillet(formerly)BuzbyRoostre(formerly)Olof MörckElize RydEnemiesIndustry ManSirusProfessor Wilx(currently)Mouse Fitzgerald(currently)Eye(currently)Golden Joe(currently)Skillet(currently)Roostre(currently)The Kid(currently)StatusAliveVoiced ByMary Spender Ariais a major protagonist who was introduced in thethird seasonof12 oz. Mouse. She is a British scientist who operates alarge laboratorywith a man namedProfessor Wilx. They currently live on a new world calledLotharganin. Being the top two scientists, Aria and Wilx were once recruited byShyd Industriesto work on a confidential project, but are now stuck in a series of simulated universes, trying to reroute their way back to Outer Earth. She was voiced by British singer/songwriterMary Spender, who collaborated alongsideMatt MaiellaroonFreebird Your Mind, a song heard during the closing credits of "Francis, Cheap & Out of Control". Due to Mary's love for music, this aspect of her life was incorporated into the character, with Aria often seen playing an acoustic guitar andsinging.

characters.name: Baby Mouse
characters.description: Baby MouseSpeciesMouseGenderFemaleAlliancesMouse Fitzgerald(father)Woman Mouse(mother)EnemiesThe Shadowy FigureStatusUnknown Woman Mouse(mother) Baby Mouseis the daughter ofMouse FitzgeraldandWoman Mouse. The baby is known as a girl due to the pronouns that Fitz uses for his child when asking an unknown doctor about her ("Is she supposed to be that small"?), and only appears in Fitz's dream/flashback sequences[1][2]. As a baby, she lays in a crib and only communicates through screeching and crying.

characters.name: Black Beast
characters.description: Black BeastSpeciesSnake-like monsterGenderMaleStatusAlive TheBlack Beastis a monstrous beast who stars as the title character of a monster movie with the same name. He appears as a brief flashing image throughout the12 oz. Mouseseries, on a screen of static or a background that is either red, white or transparent. Anytime Black Beast ever appears onscreen, he makes a lion-like roar and flashes to black (hence the name of the beast). According toFitz, the beast's movie was worked on by aSFX Guy. The Black Beast is a white monstrous snake-like creature with a mustache, a hairdo, eyes, eyebrows, a mouth, sharp teeth, and fangs. He also has three small white colored blood drips above his mouth and fangs, curly lines on his snake-like body, and some sort of dorsal fin on the bottom of the aforementioned latter. He'll often flash from white to black.

characters.name: Bow-tie Bots
characters.description: Bow-Tie BotsSpeciesBow-Tie RobotsAlliancesSharkRectangular BusinessmanClockHovervacsEnemiesThe whole townMouse FitzgeraldStatusDeceasedTheBow-Tie Bots, referred to as theTie-Bots, are small, robotic bowties that were built byShark. They usually appear as regular non-sentient bowties that can transform themselves into killer robots that wield tiny laser cannons. They originally appeared as a part of Shark's simulation in "Bowtime", but serve a much more larger role in the last couple episodes of thesecond seasonas they were released by Shark, along with theHovervacs, to wreak havoc all overthe town.Mouse Fitzgeraldhas often fought them (usually with his friends) on several occasions such as in "Bowtime", "Pre-Reckoning", the "Invictus" special, and theSeason 3episode "Because They Could". Rectangular BusinessmanClockHovervacs Mouse Fitzgerald

characters.name: Bug
characters.description: BugSpeciesRobotic insectAlliancesSharkRectangular BusinessmanProntoEnemiesMouse FitzgeraldSkilletCyber Green-Sweatered WomanStatusAlive TheBug, also known as theRobot Bug, is a small robotic insect who works forShark. It often moves by crawling or spinning around on the walls of buildings such asShark's Officeand emits odd beeping sounds. The Bug is a robotic, beige colored insect with six stick-fingered legs, an oval shaped body, a small circle shaped head with two stick-fingered antennas, two small circle shaped eyes attached to its head, and a few curly lines on its back.

characters.name: Buttermilk
characters.description: ButtermilkSpeciesHumanGenderMaleAlliancesUnnamed FriendHuman CitizensStatusDeceasedVoiced ByMatt HarriganAppearances"Adventure Mouse"Buttermilkis aHuman Citizenwho appeared in theSeason 1finale "Adventure Mouse". He was one of the many identical townspeople who attendedShark's party, and got nagged by theGreen-Sweatered Woman. Sometime later, Buttermilk and a friend were admiring his brand new car until they're immediately set on fire and blown away whenFitz'sJet-car, piloted byGolden Joe, crashes onto the streets. Human Citizens Buttermilk shares the same design as one of the several generic Human Citizens (with the same hands that carry beer bottle, a mouth, and soulless eyes) who appear throughout theseries, mainly the ones with the light skin and light brown hair who wear green shirts, blue pants, gray shoes, and black bowties, which are reused again in "Bowtime" and "Francis, Cheap & Out of Control" respectively.

characters.name: Buzby
characters.description: BuzbySpeciesBeeGenderMaleAlliancesMouse FitzgeraldSkilletPeanut CopGolden JoeRoostreNew GuyMan-WomanEyeAriaElize RydOlof MörckThe KidEnemiesSharkRectangular BusinessmanIndustry ManSpiderSmall Dancing Man(presumably)StatusAliveVoiced ByDana Snyder(Invictus)Paul Painter(Season 3)Buzby(also known asThe ExterminatorinSeason 3) is a bee exterminator who made his first appearance in the12 oz. Mousespecial "Invictus". He was originally hired byMouse Fitzgeraldto kill a giganticSpiderresiding within hishome, using a chain-gun. He later saved Fitz fromShark, triggered his memory, swapped his mind with Shark's, and helped him escape fromQ109, but he was ultimately left behind in theCardboard CitywithSkilletin Season 3. They would later join Fitz and co. to help on taking out theIndustry ManandShyd Industriesby the end of the season. He was originally voiced byDana Snyderin the "Invictus" special, but due to unknown circumstances, the role was reprised byPaul Painterfor the third season. SkilletPeanut CopGolden JoeRoostreNew GuyMan-WomanEyeAriaElize RydOlof MörckThe Kid Rectangular BusinessmanIndustry ManSpiderSmall Dancing Man(presumably)

characters.name: Characters
characters.description: There are a wide variety of characters featured in12 oz. Mouse, ranging from talking animals, anthropomorphic appendages, ordinary humans, robots, and more bizarre creatures. This list documents every character that has been featured in12 oz. Mouse, whether their role is minor or major. Protagonistsare the characters that the plot of a series centers around and follows. In most cases, they try to stop the antagonist's malicious plans. The following is a list of both major and minor protagonists in12 oz. Mouse. Antagonistsare considered to be villains in a series or evildoers. They are used in a story to hinder the protagonist's actions or plans in some way or form. The following list is an overview of the major and minor antagonists featured in12 oz. Mouse. Neutral charactersare considered to be characters who have no true opinions and don't interfere with the plot in a major way. This also includes very minor characters, which are those who do not interfere or change the storyline. The following is a list of neutral and very minor characters that appear in12 oz. Mouse.

characters.name: CJ Muff
characters.description: CJ Muff/BirminghamSpeciesPossibly human before; now an orbGenderMaleAlliancesRoostreLiquorMouse FitzgeraldBuzbyEnemiesSharkRectangular BusinessmanClockStatusAliveVoiced ByMatt Maiellaro(Season 2)George Lowe(Season 3) George Lowe(Season 3) Birmingham, also known asCJ Muff, is a mysterious person who is mentioned multiple times throughout the12 oz. Mouseseries.Roostreexplains that CJ was able to escapeQ109, and he was one of his closest friends. He finally appears in "Farewell", as he is shown to be a glowing orb, resemblingArcheus. His form before he escapedQ109is unknown and never shown. CJ Muff would later speak on a monitor in "First 12", the first episode ofSeason 3, about him experiencing a bizarre dream. According to Roostre, CJ Muff's real name is actuallyBirmingham.

His form before he escapedQ109is unknown and never shown. CJ Muff would later speak on a monitor in "First 12", the first episode ofSeason 3, about him experiencing a bizarre dream. According to Roostre, CJ Muff's real name is actuallyBirmingham.

characters.name: Corn-Droid
characters.description: Corn-DroidSpeciesCorndog Robot/DroidGenderMale (presumably)AlliancesRoostre(creator)LiquorHuman CitizensEnemiesHovervacsStatusAliveTheCorn-Droidis a robotic weaponized corndog shaped droid that was built byRoostre. It is hidden in thesewersand has a pad with a switch (which is a black square with red glowing lines and an outline of a five fingered hand with a red glowing circle on it) and a garage door that covers the droid itself. It is required for Roostre to have his hominghandto activate it (via pressing a switch on its pad) as he wanted to summon the droid upon finding out that hisCorn Dog Farmwasdestroyed. The purpose of the droid, upon being activated and released by Roostre after receiving his missing hand[1], is to serve as a weapon who can blast and destroy large swarms ofShark'sHovervacs, from sucking uptownspeoplearound thedestroyed city, with a plethora of guns that can come out of its stomach[2]. LiquorHuman Citizens According for Roostre, he described the droid as a skeleton with an "array of future weaponry" surrounded by meat that is "mechanically separated" and "coated in a teflon batter and dried fried", whose only mission is to "wipe this freakin' place out" on his own command[3].

characters.name: Desert Monster
characters.description: Desert MonsterSpeciesMonsterGenderMaleEnemiesThe KidRoostreStatusDeceasedAppearancesBecause They CouldTheDesert Monsteris adesertdwelling monster who appeared in theSeason 3episode "Because They Could". He shows up, afterRoostreis told to duck, and screeches atThe Kid, who immediately kills him with a beam from an eyeball-like weapon. Upon death, the monster's heart comes out, suddenly grows bat-like wings, and flies away. Roostre The Desert Monster is a heavily detailed monster with greenish and yellowish arms and hands, a ribcage-like body, a realistic heart (that is visible in the middle of his body, and has the abilities of growing wings and flying away), closed eyes, fangs, and a large mouth respectively.

characters.name: Eye
characters.description: EyeSpeciesEyeballGenderMaleAlliancesMouse FitzgeraldSkilletNew GuyRhodaLiquorGolden JoeSecond Eye(formerly)Aria(formerly)Professor Wilx(formerly)Elize RydOlof MörckRoostreThe KidEnemiesSharkRectangular BusinessmanClock/Industry ManSecond EyeAria(currently)Professor Wilx(currently)StatusAliveVoiced ByNick Ingkatanuwat TheEyeis a giant, friendly eyeball who speaks in a calm and almost monotone pace, but will always emphasize on saying words or letters that sound similar to the word of "eye" (such as the letter I). He mainly lives on a smallisland, in the middle of the ocean, which he claims is in either Iowa (pronounced as Eye-owa by him) or Wyoming (pronounced as W-eye-oming by him). The Eye is a giant, crudely drawn eyeball with two stick-finger legs and feet, a large pink eyelid with seven eyelashes, a large blue pupil, an iris, and a mouth. He also has a small white eyelid that can blink, similar to his large pink eyelid.

characters.name: Fire Breathing Snake
characters.description: Fire Breathing SnakeSpeciesSnakeEnemiesRectangular BusinessmanStatusAlive TheFire Breathing Snakeis a green snake with yellow eyes who lives insideRhodaand has the ability to breathe fire at will. The Fire Breathing Snake was first revealed to the audience in "Adventure Mouse" after Rhoda was telepathically sliced in half by theRectangular Businessman. After all of Rhoda's vital organs are shown, we see the snake, curled inside Rhoda's body. After being seen by the businessman, it angrily breathes flames and slithers away. How it got itself into Rhoda's body is unknown.

characters.name: Golden Joe
characters.description: Golden "Joe"SpeciesUnknown mouse like orb being (presumably a mouse)GenderMaleAlliancesMouse FitzgeraldSkilletPeanut Cop(best friend)RoostreRhodaLiquorEyeKiki(love interest)AriaElize RydOlof MörckThe KidEnemiesSharkRectangular BusinessmanClock/Industry ManSecond EyeHandPineapplesStatusAliveVoiced ByVishal RoneyKeith Crofford (theoriginal pilotonly) Golden Joeis a loud-mouthed, close friend ofMouse Fitzgerald. He is a rapper who is capable of teleportation, allowing him to teleport from place to place. His voice is distorted by a reverb effect and constantly shouts instead of speaking more softly. He often irritates people around him,Roostrein particular, due to his use of long and verbose sentences. Golden Joe is a somewhat well-known rapper, asRhodarecognized him and couldn't believe the latter was in hisbar. As a songwriter, Joe places a lot of importance on his ability to rhyme. It is unknown where Golden Joe's ability of transportation originates.Sharkregards him to be "all disappeary and shit" because of this power. Golden Joe is a green orb-like being with two lime green mouse-like ears on both sides of his head and a golden tooth in his mouth that has a dollar sign, marked in white, on it.

characters.description: Golden "Joe"SpeciesUnknown mouse like orb being (presumably a mouse)GenderMaleAlliancesMouse FitzgeraldSkilletPeanut Cop(best friend)RoostreRhodaLiquorEyeKiki(love interest)AriaElize RydOlof MörckThe KidEnemiesSharkRectangular BusinessmanClock/Industry ManSecond EyeHandPineapplesStatusAliveVoiced ByVishal RoneyKeith Crofford (theoriginal pilotonly) Golden Joeis a loud-mouthed, close friend ofMouse Fitzgerald. He is a rapper who is capable of teleportation, allowing him to teleport from place to place. His voice is distorted by a reverb effect and constantly shouts instead of speaking more softly. He often irritates people around him,Roostrein particular, due to his use of long and verbose sentences. Golden Joe is a somewhat well-known rapper, asRhodarecognized him and couldn't believe the latter was in hisbar. As a songwriter, Joe places a lot of importance on his ability to rhyme. It is unknown where Golden Joe's ability of transportation originates.Sharkregards him to be "all disappeary and shit" because of this power. Golden Joe is a green orb-like being with two lime green mouse-like ears on both sides of his head and a golden tooth in his mouth that has a dollar sign, marked in white, on it.

characters.name: Green-Sweatered Woman
characters.description: Green-Sweatered WomanSpeciesHumanCyborgGenderFemaleAlliancesMouse FitzgeraldSkilletLiquorProducer ManGolden JoePeanut CopAriaSirusElize RydOlof MörckEnemiesSharkRectangular BusinessmanIndustry Man/ClockSecond EyeBowtie BotsHovervacsHuman CitizensStatusAliveVoiced ByMelissa Warrenburg TheGreen-Sweatered Womanis a woman who usually speaks in a very annoying and repetitive manner. She often nags to people and tells them on what they're not allowed to do, ranging from ordinarytownspeopletoMouse Fitzgerald, who seems to find her annoying. She would later be telepathically sliced in half by theRectangular Businessmanand revived as a cyborg bySkillet(when Mouse requested him to make her into a "terminator"). As a cyborg, she joinedFitzand Skillet's side as their walking arsenal, carrying many of the guns/firearms fromRoostre's weapon cache, often helping the two in gunfights. Outside ofQ109, the Green-Sweatered Woman has a nurse persona of herself in the real world, as revealed at theendof the second season. She is currently back to normal self as of thethird season, and now works withthe Architect. The Green-Sweatered Woman is a crudely drawn woman with blonde bobbed hair, an eye, a nose, a mouth, an arm, and a three fingered hand who wears a green blouse/sweater, a pink skirt, and two green shoes on her feet. As a nurse, she wears a white nurse hat glasses green shoes and a white V-Neck scrub gown with a skirt.

characters.name: Hand
characters.description: The HandSpeciesHandGenderMaleAlliancesRoostre(owner)Producer ManCorn-DroidEnemiesProntoPeanut CopGolden JoeLiquorMouse FitzgeraldEyeStatusAliveTheHandis a small, severed hand with four fingers (one of which is a thumb) who originally belonged toRoostre. He was eventually separated from Roostre and replaced with a hook at some point before theseriesbegan. As a severed hand, he can move around using his fingers. Producer ManCorn-Droid Peanut CopGolden JoeLiquorMouse FitzgeraldEye

characters.name: Hovervacs
characters.description: HovervacsSpeciesDevilish-like robotsGenderMaleAlliancesSharkRectangular BusinessmanBowtie BotsEnemiesThe whole townCorn-DroidStatusExtantHovervacsare robotic nuisances who were built byShark. They have the ability to suck objects and beings up by using two vacuum-like tubes on each side of their body. Their main purpose is to obtainaspirinand to suck up anyone that tries to stop them. They are one of the main reasons why thetownis turned into chaos within the last couple episodes of thesecond season, alongside theBowtie Bots. They appear and make cameos in multiple episodes throughout theseriesbut play more of major role in the last couple episodes of Season 2. Rectangular BusinessmanBowtie Bots Corn-Droid

characters.name: Human Citizens
characters.description: Human CitizensSpeciesHumanGenderMaleFemale (Mothers of Babies)AlliancesMouse FitzgeraldSkilletMan-WomanFrancisGolden JoeKikiEnemiesSharkRectangular BusinessmanClockGreen-Sweatered WomanBow-tie BotsHovervacsStatusAliveVoiced ByMatt Maiellaro(Buttermilk's friend inAdventure Mouse)Ted MurphyThom FooleryAndrew Quitmeyer(Mother of Baby inMeat Warrior)Pierre CerratoBrad Lee ZimmermanTheHuman Citizensare the local townspeople ofCardboard City. They are unnamed civilians who frequently appear as background characters throughout the12 oz. Mouseseries. One of the most notable citizens to appear in the series is namedButtermilk, who appeared in theSeason 1episode "Adventure Mouse". Some of them are shown to have jobs/occupations in the city as its implied that they work forSharkand theRectangular Businessman, as shown in"Rememorized", and one of them presumably runs the town's localGas Station, as shown in"Meaty Dreamy". Furthermore, in the episode "Meat Warrior", we see a citizen as a construction man/worker, working on a utility pole before being electrocuted to death byPeanut Cop. While the citizens are mainly background/minor characters, they do sometimes have a few speaking lines here and there, and are often killed throughout the series. Female (Mothers of Babies) SkilletMan-WomanFrancisGolden JoeKiki

characters.name: Human Eye
characters.description: Human EyeSpeciesHumanGenderMaleStatusAliveVoiced ByNick IngkatanuwatAppearances"Prolegomenon" (only appearance) TheHuman Eye(also known asHuman Eye Number 1) is a human version of theEyefrom the real world whose eyeballs are connected to a machine in asimulation program. He appeared once in theSeason 2episode "Prolegomenon" and said that he was tired of this and asked to leave now. The Human Eye is a heavily detailed man with pale white skin, a nose, a mouth, and orange hair who wears a white shirt, withSkilletand a drum set on it, that is mostly covered by a cyan jacket, blue jeans, and brown shoes respectively. Both of his eyes are never seen as their covered by a machine.

characters.name: Human Eye Number 2
characters.description: Human Eye Number 2SpeciesHumanGenderMaleAlliancesMouse FitzgeraldChildrenStatusAliveVoiced ByNick IngkatanuwatAppearancesInvictusTheHuman Eye Number 2is a human/kid-like version of the originalEyewho appeared at the beginning of the "Invictus" special. Instead of living on anislandin the middle of the ocean, he lives in a house from a neighborhood in the real world. He is first seen watering some flowers with a gardening hose while greeting the neighborhood children, who ride on bikes. He appears again to tellFitzthat he is very good atyo-yosince the latter can do some interesting tricks with it. Children Instead of being an eyeball, the second Human Eye has a round head, a blue torso, stick figure-like arms, short legs, a mouth, ears, eyelashes, and two large eyes with blue irises and pupils, and also wears a red baseball cap on his head. The second Human Eye, just like the original Eye, is very nice and friendly towards other people, such as the children that live in the neighborhood and Mouse Fitzgerald. He will also emphasize on saying words and letters that sound similar to the word "eye".
characters.personality_section: The second Human Eye, just like the original Eye, is very nice and friendly towards other people, such as the children that live in the neighborhood and Mouse Fitzgerald. He will also emphasize on saying words and letters that sound similar to the word "eye".
